question: A client starts a new process instance through `PublishMessage` against a message start event. According to the routing best-practice, what is the implication for version selection?

options.text: You cannot influence the version of the process definition that gets instantiated.
The publisher selects the version via a `version` field on the published message.
The oldest deployed version that declares that message is used by default.
All deployed versions of the process that declare the message are started in parallel.

a.text: The docs state plainly: 'you cannot influence the version of the process definition used when starting a process instance by message'. The trade-off for not needing the BPMN process ID is losing the ability to pin the version.

b.text: `PublishMessage` does not take a version field; version pinning is only available on `CreateProcessInstance`, where you call `.bpmnProcessId(...).version(n)`.

c.text: The engine does not fall back to the oldest version — the docs simply say the publisher has no influence over which version is used.

d.text: A published message starts at most one new instance per matching subscription; the engine does not fan out one publication to every deployed version.

explanation: Starting by message via `PublishMessage` frees the client from knowing the BPMN process ID, but the trade-off, as the docs spell out, is that the client cannot influence which version of the process definition is used. If version selection matters, you must instead call `CreateProcessInstance` with `.bpmnProcessId(...).version(n)`.

docs.title: Routing events to processes - best practices
